message:
feat(trace): add adversarial regime-shift synthesizer contract (AA-06.4), close AA-06 epic

4-file contract bead for AA-06.4: 7 search objectives (tail-SLO,
fairness, authority, recovery, wake-loss, obligation-leak,
diagnosability), 21 mutation axes with budget constraints, challenge
corpus promotion with feedback loop. 27-test invariant suite covering
objective coverage, mutation axis consistency, deterministic seeding,
budget enforcement, promotion criteria, and minimization.

Closes AA-06 epic (all 4 children + 3 superseded closed).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.5.4","title":"[AA-05.1] Design session-typed obligation and protocol surface","description":"Design the opt-in session/typestate surface for the obligation-rich parts of Asupersync: permits, acks, leases, reserve/commit channels, and similar multi-phase protocols.","design":"Scope:\n- Identify the highest-value protocol families to type first.\n- Specify how the typed surface coexists with the current dynamic APIs and macros.\n- Keep ergonomics practical enough for real adoption.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Protocol states and transitions are explicit for at least one important family.
2. Design shows how typed and dynamic surfaces interoperate without ambiguity.
3. Compile-time constraints, runtime oracle complements, and planned compile-fail plus migration test surfaces are documented together rather than split across future beads.
4. Migration notes explain where the typed surface should and should not be used first, what developer-ergonomics risks remain, and which structured diagnostics or log fields will make adoption debuggable.","notes":"Claimed after handing back overlapping AA-01.1 core surface; taking AA-05.1 session-typed obligation/protocol design slice.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"JadeGrove","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:52:49.353299375Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:54:22.126540271Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T06:54:22.126521005Z","close_reason":"Implemented code-backed session-typed obligation adoption contract with explicit protocol states/transitions, typed-vs-dynamic interoperability notes, runtime oracle complements, migration test surfaces, diagnostics fields, and focused passing tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa05-static-correctness","alien-artifact","correctness","runtime-ascension","types"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.5.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:52:49.353299375Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.5.5","title":"[AA-05.2] Prototype static leak checker and graded-budget pilot on restricted surfaces","description":"Implement the first restricted static safety tools for Asupersync: a leak checker that flags likely unresolved obligations and a graded-budget pilot that proves resource tracking is viable on a narrow surface.","design":"Scope:\n- Use a deliberately restricted IR or API slice rather than promising universal coverage.\n- Ensure diagnostics are deterministic and understandable.\n- Demonstrate at least one compile-time or analysis-time catch that would otherwise rely on runtime discovery.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The leak checker or graded-budget pilot catches real misuse patterns on its target surface.
2. Diagnostics are deterministic and include stable locations, obligation kinds, remediation hints, and machine-readable codes for logs or CI.
3. Unit tests and deterministic E2E sample programs cover both sound positive detections and important non-findings.
4. Prototype outputs are clearly bounded: what is covered, what is intentionally out of scope, what still needs runtime enforcement, and how a user should interpret the result.","notes":"Primary risk: false confidence from partial analysis.\nCountermeasure: state scope and conservatism explicitly in artifact output.\nFallback: runtime oracles remain the authority for uncovered cases.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:52:54.356870025Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:13:50.528452860Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T07:13:50.528434346Z","close_reason":"Implemented restricted static leak-checker pilot metadata: machine-readable codes, deterministic locations, remediation hints, explicit coverage contract, and conservative graded-budget summary; validated with focused unit and integration tests via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa05-static-correctness","alien-artifact","formal","runtime-ascension","types"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.5.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:52:54.356870025Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.5.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:54:57.980240638Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.5.6","title":"[AA-05.3] Validate compile-fail, unit, E2E, and migration behavior for static safety surfaces","description":"Explicit validation bead for the static-safety track: compile-fail suites, unit tests, E2E migration scenarios, and structured logging proving the new surfaces help rather than confuse users.","design":"Scope:\n- Add compile-fail tests for leaks/protocol misuse on the typed surface.\n- Run migration scenarios that compare typed and dynamic APIs.\n- Capture operator/developer diagnostics as structured artifacts, not just compiler output.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Compile-fail suites are deterministic and explain the targeted failure mode.\n2. Unit and E2E tests show typed and dynamic paths agree on valid behavior.\n3. Structured logs and docs provide direct migration guidance and troubleshooting hints.\n4. If ergonomics or coverage are insufficient, the surface stays explicitly experimental rather than quietly becoming default.","notes":"Primary risk: validation focuses only on correctness, not usability.\nCountermeasure: include migration scenarios and diagnostic quality requirements.\nFallback: leave the static surface opt-in and document the dynamic path as canonical.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldTower","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:52:59.339168842Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:33:54.978413229Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T07:33:54.978389695Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa05-static-correctness","alien-artifact","runtime-ascension","testing","types"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.5.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:52:59.339168842Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.5.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.5.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:00.485102137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-1508v.6","title":"[AA-06] Concurrency Intelligence 2.0","description":"Turn the runtime's advanced trace and topology ideas into a cost-capped, operator-useful intelligence layer: minimal nondeterminism capture, canonical traces, scalable exploration, and better debugging surfaces.","design":"Background:
- Asupersync already has unusually sophisticated replay and exploration ingredients.
- The next step is operational scale: make those mechanisms explicit about budgets, artifacts, and when to degrade to simpler heuristics.
- A static validation corpus is not enough for a runtime this ambitious; the track should also manufacture new challenge cases as it learns where the system is fragile.

Track outputs:
- Hindsight logging contract for minimal nondeterminism capture.
- Cost-capped topology-guided exploration and canonicalization.
- Validation corpus for replay, minimization, and distributed inconsistency debugging.
- Adversarial regime-shift synthesizer and challenge-corpus feedback loop.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Trace capture explicitly distinguishes required nondeterminism from derivable state.
2. Exploration and scoring algorithms have stated time, memory, and search caps and deterministic fallback behavior.
3. Canonical traces, minimized counterexamples, and inconsistency diagnostics are available as first-class artifacts.
4. Validation proves these tools improve debugging value without undermining replay determinism.
5. The track ships deterministic unit and E2E replay suites, structured debugging artifacts, and exact rerun commands so users get better diagnostics instead of more opaque tooling.
6. The track can synthesize budgeted challenge workloads that feed back into the corpus, CI, and closure process rather than freezing the test surface in place.","notes":"Recommendation card:\n- Change: make trace intelligence practical rather than merely impressive.\n- EV: high for debugging leverage, proof evidence, and schedule-exploration ROI.\n- Primary risk: exponential blowups or opaque heuristics.\n- Countermeasure: explicit caps, fallback scoring, and artifactized outputs.\n- Conservative fallback: plain deterministic replay and current DPOR surfaces remain available.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:44:40.064119891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:57.742989156Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa06-trace-intelligence","alien-artifact","determinism","dpor","runtime-ascension","trace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:44:40.064119891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:45:13.054090765Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- The next step is operational scale: make those mechanisms explicit about budgets, artifacts, and when to degrade to simpler heuristics.

Track outputs:

 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.6.1","title":"[SUPERSEDED] [AA-06.1] Define hindsight logging and minimal nondeterminism capture contract","description":"Define exactly which nondeterministic inputs must be recorded, which state is derivable, and how to emit compact replay artifacts that preserve Asupersync's deterministic debugging advantages without waste.","design":"Scope:\n- Enumerate scheduler choices, external timing, entropy, I/O ordering, and other true nondeterminism sources.\n- Distinguish them from derivable state that should not be redundantly logged.\n- Produce a versioned artifact contract and cost budget.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The contract names required nondeterminism sources and excluded derived state.\n2. Replay artifacts are versioned, compact, and tied to workload/correlation IDs.\n3. Unit tests protect serialization and deterministic reconstruction boundaries.\n4. Structured logs make capture/replay coverage explicit so missing sources cannot hide silently.","notes":"Superseded by asupersync-1508v.6.4 after an interrupted shell pass created a duplicate task. Keep this issue only as audit history; the active canonical bead is asupersync-1508v.6.4.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:50:15.061494443Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:05.077369927Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:05.077247971Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa06-trace-intelligence","alien-artifact","determinism","runtime-ascension","trace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:50:15.061494443Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.6.2","title":"[SUPERSEDED] [AA-06.2] Implement cost-capped topology-guided exploration and canonical trace artifacts","description":"Implement the next-step trace-intelligence path: topology-guided or canonicalized exploration with explicit time/memory/search caps and deterministic fallback heuristics when the math gets too expensive.","design":"Scope:\n- Combine DPOR, canonicalization, and topological scoring only where they improve search yield.\n- Make every expensive step budgeted and observable.\n- Emit operator-usable artifacts rather than opaque scores.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Exploration/canonicalization algorithms expose caps and exact exhaustion behavior.\n2. Artifacts include canonical fingerprints, chosen heuristic path, and fallback reason when used.\n3. Benchmarks compare bug-finding/debugging ROI against simpler deterministic replay baselines.\n4. Unit tests protect canonicalization stability and cap-trigger behavior.","notes":"Superseded by asupersync-1508v.6.5 after an interrupted shell pass created a duplicate task. Keep this issue only as audit history; the active canonical bead is asupersync-1508v.6.5.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:50:19.988597530Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:09.788130708Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:09.788023859Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa06-trace-intelligence","alien-artifact","dpor","runtime-ascension","trace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:50:19.988597530Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.6.3","title":"[SUPERSEDED] [AA-06.3] Validate replay minimization, counterexample quality, and distributed inconsistency debugging","description":"Explicit validation bead for the trace-intelligence track: prove the new artifacts yield better minimized counterexamples, reproducible replay, and useful distributed inconsistency diagnostics without sacrificing determinism.","design":"Scope:\n- Build a deterministic validation corpus with known concurrency bugs and inconsistency shapes.\n- Compare minimized/canonical outputs against existing replay surfaces.\n- Include distributed/saga-style inconsistency cases where sheaf-style obstruction diagnostics matter.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Deterministic unit and E2E scenarios prove replay equivalence after canonicalization/minimization.\n2. Counterexample artifacts are smaller or clearer on the validation corpus, with comparator evidence.\n3. Structured logs include capture IDs, canonical fingerprints, and obstruction/minimization metadata.\n4. Any unexplained replay drift hard-fails the track.","notes":"Superseded by asupersync-1508v.6.6 after an interrupted shell pass created a duplicate task. Keep this issue only as audit history; the active canonical bead is asupersync-1508v.6.6.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:50:24.901615627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:14.535337070Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:14.535189946Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa06-trace-intelligence","alien-artifact","runtime-ascension","testing","trace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:50:24.901615627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.6.4","title":"[AA-06.1] Define hindsight logging and minimal nondeterminism capture contract","description":"Define exactly which nondeterministic inputs must be recorded, which state is derivable, and how to emit compact replay artifacts that preserve Asupersync's deterministic debugging advantages without waste.","design":"Scope:\n- Enumerate scheduler choices, external timing, entropy, I/O ordering, and other true nondeterminism sources.\n- Distinguish them from derivable state that should not be redundantly logged.\n- Produce a versioned artifact contract and cost budget.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The contract names required nondeterminism sources and excluded derived state.
2. Replay artifacts are versioned, compact, and tied to workload/correlation IDs.
3. Deterministic unit tests and deterministic capture/replay E2E smoke scripts protect serialization and reconstruction boundaries.
4. Structured logs make capture/replay coverage, excluded-state reasoning, and missing-source failures explicit so gaps cannot hide silently.","notes":"Primary risk: logging too much or too little.\nCountermeasure: make the contract explicit and test derivability assumptions.\nFallback: retain current fuller trace path where minimal capture is not yet safe.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:04.425194297Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T17:37:40.503704419Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T17:37:40.503680715Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa06-trace-intelligence","alien-artifact","determinism","runtime-ascension","trace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:02.973486788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:04.425194297Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.6.5","title":"[AA-06.2] Implement cost-capped topology-guided exploration and canonical trace artifacts","description":"Implement the next-step trace-intelligence path: topology-guided or canonicalized exploration with explicit time, memory, and search caps plus deterministic fallback heuristics when the math gets too expensive.","design":"Scope:\n- Combine DPOR, canonicalization, and topological scoring only where they improve search yield.\n- Make every expensive step budgeted and observable.\n- Emit operator-usable artifacts rather than opaque scores.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Exploration/canonicalization algorithms expose caps and exact exhaustion behavior.
2. Artifacts include canonical fingerprints, chosen heuristic path, and fallback reason when used.
3. Deterministic E2E exploration runs and benchmarks compare bug-finding and debugging ROI against simpler deterministic replay baselines.
4. Unit tests and structured logs protect canonicalization stability, cap-trigger behavior, and operator-visible explanation of why a path was explored or skipped.","notes":"Primary risk: complex heuristics that are hard to reason about.\nCountermeasure: explicit budgets, stable tie-breaks, and artifactized decisions.\nFallback: deterministic replay plus simpler DPOR scoring remains available.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:09.463954745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T17:48:51.001157196Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T17:48:51.001133101Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa06-trace-intelligence","alien-artifact","dpor","runtime-ascension","trace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:09.463954745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:05.478561902Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.6.6","title":"[AA-06.3] Validate replay minimization, counterexample quality, and distributed inconsistency debugging","description":"Explicit validation bead for the trace-intelligence track: prove the new artifacts yield better minimized counterexamples, reproducible replay, and useful distributed inconsistency diagnostics without sacrificing determinism.","design":"Scope:\n- Build a deterministic validation corpus with known concurrency bugs and inconsistency shapes.\n- Compare minimized/canonical outputs against existing replay surfaces.\n- Include distributed and saga-style inconsistency cases where obstruction diagnostics matter.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Deterministic unit and E2E scenarios prove replay equivalence after canonicalization/minimization.\n2. Counterexample artifacts are smaller or clearer on the validation corpus, with comparator evidence.\n3. Structured logs include capture IDs, canonical fingerprints, and obstruction/minimization metadata.\n4. Any unexplained replay drift hard-fails the track.","notes":"Primary risk: nicer-looking traces that are semantically wrong.\nCountermeasure: equivalence checks and replay round-trips are mandatory.\nFallback: current replay/counterexample path remains canonical until this bead passes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:14.520104574Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T18:00:19.211473042Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T18:00:19.211447354Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa06-trace-intelligence","alien-artifact","runtime-ascension","testing","trace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:14.520104574Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:08.059088474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-1508v.6.7","title":"[AA-06.4] Build adversarial regime-shift synthesizer and challenge-corpus feedback loop","description":"Create a deterministic, budgeted search that synthesizes new worst-case workloads and regime shifts from replay traces, control models, and invariants, then feeds those cases back into the challenge corpus used by the rest of the program.","design":"Scope:
- Use replay traces, control-seam inventory, and digital-twin or invariant objectives to search for high-value new workloads rather than relying only on hand-authored scenarios.
- Mutate arrival patterns, cancellation timing, wakeup patterns, retry pressure, network conditions, authority transitions, and crash timing under explicit compute budgets.
- Emit minimized challenge cases as first-class workload manifests with explanations of which user-visible failure mode or risk they target.
- Keep the search deterministic, budgeted, and auditable; this is not a black-box fuzzing escape hatch.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The synthesizer is deterministic, budgeted, and emits challenge workloads together with minimized repros, objective values, structured logs, and exact replay commands.
2. Search objectives include tail-SLO breach, fairness drift, authority or recovery edge exposure, and diagnosability loss, each tied to explicit invariants or loss terms.
3. Deterministic unit tests and deterministic E2E challenge-promotion drills verify that generated cases are reproducible, minimized correctly, and promoted into a versioned challenge corpus with human-readable rationale and machine-readable metadata.
4. Any generated case that reveals a blind spot feeds back into comparator tables, composition validation, CI, and closure artifacts instead of remaining an orphan result.","notes":"Why this bead matters:
- A static corpus eventually teaches the runtime to pass yesterday's tests.
- The alien-artifact move is to make the system adversarially self-hardening: use its own models and traces to manufacture tomorrow's failures before users find them.
- This is accretive because every good synthesized case becomes permanent evidence and regression protection for every later release.

Guardrails:
- Search must be deterministic and capped.
- Every promoted case must be inspectable by a human, not just scored by an optimizer.
- If a case is interesting but not reproducible, it is a bug in the synthesizer, not a win.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:40.008550726Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:17:14.933295809Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa06-trace-intelligence","alien-artifact","control","determinism","runtime-ascension","tail-latency","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:45.070259119Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:47.594073450Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:50.109541934Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:40.008550726Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:52.639693209Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"asupersync-1508v.6.7","title":"[AA-06.4] Build adversarial regime-shift synthesizer and challenge-corpus feedback loop","description":"Create a deterministic, budgeted search that synthesizes new worst-case workloads and regime shifts from replay traces, control models, and invariants, then feeds those cases back into the challenge corpus used by the rest of the program.","design":"Scope:
- Use replay traces, control-seam inventory, and digital-twin or invariant objectives to search for high-value new workloads rather than relying only on hand-authored scenarios.
- Mutate arrival patterns, cancellation timing, wakeup patterns, retry pressure, network conditions, authority transitions, and crash timing under explicit compute budgets.
- Emit minimized challenge cases as first-class workload manifests with explanations of which user-visible failure mode or risk they target.
- Keep the search deterministic, budgeted, and auditable; this is not a black-box fuzzing escape hatch.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The synthesizer is deterministic, budgeted, and emits challenge workloads together with minimized repros, objective values, structured logs, and exact replay commands.
2. Search objectives include tail-SLO breach, fairness drift, authority or recovery edge exposure, and diagnosability loss, each tied to explicit invariants or loss terms.
3. Deterministic unit tests and deterministic E2E challenge-promotion drills verify that generated cases are reproducible, minimized correctly, and promoted into a versioned challenge corpus with human-readable rationale and machine-readable metadata.
4. Any generated case that reveals a blind spot feeds back into comparator tables, composition validation, CI, and closure artifacts instead of remaining an orphan result.","notes":"Why this bead matters:
- A static corpus eventually teaches the runtime to pass yesterday's tests.
- The alien-artifact move is to make the system adversarially self-hardening: use its own models and traces to manufacture tomorrow's failures before users find them.
- This is accretive because every good synthesized case becomes permanent evidence and regression protection for every later release.

Guardrails:
- Search must be deterministic and capped.
- Every promoted case must be inspectable by a human, not just scored by an optimizer.
- If a case is interesting but not reproducible, it is a bug in the synthesizer, not a win.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:40.008550726Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T20:35:03.676224900Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T20:35:03.676199963Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa06-trace-intelligence","alien-artifact","control","determinism","runtime-ascension","tail-latency","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:45.070259119Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:47.594073450Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:50.109541934Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:40.008550726Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:52.639693209Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.7","title":"[AA-07] Authority Plane and Controller Sandboxing","description":"Extend Asupersync's capability-security story across distributed authority, adaptive controller execution, and delegated runtime decisions without introducing ambient power.","design":"Core idea:\n- If controller logic or distributed coordination gains hidden authority, the entire alien-artifact story collapses.\n- This track makes authority flow explicit, attenuable, sandboxable, revocable, and auditable.\n\nTrack outputs:\n- Distributed capability token/delegation model.\n- Sandboxed controller execution membrane.\n- Authority-flow validation and revocation doctrine.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Distributed or delegated capabilities have explicit attenuation, expiry, caveat, and audit semantics.
2. Controller execution occurs inside a resource-capped membrane that cannot escape ambient authority.

4. Fallback remains local/static authority when distributed/controller authority cannot be validated.
5. The track leaves deterministic unit and E2E abuse suites, structured audit logs, and exact denial/recovery commands so authority failures are explainable and safe for users.","notes":"Recommendation card:\n- Change: treat authority flow as rigorously as scheduling.\n- EV: high because it protects every other advanced track from becoming an unsafe hidden-control channel.\n- Primary risk: distributed capability systems becoming operationally brittle.\n- Countermeasure: attenuation-first design, strong audit trails, and default-local fallback.\n- Conservative fallback: current local Cx semantics remain the baseline operating mode.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:44:45.274736635Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:57:07.364892675Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa07-authority","alien-artifact","capability","runtime-ascension","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:44:45.274736635Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:45:15.531165399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+{
+  "contract_version": "adversarial-regime-shift-v1",

+  "runner_script": "scripts/run_adversarial_regime_shift_smoke.sh",

+  "runner_report_schema_version": "adversarial-regime-shift-smoke-run-report-v1",
+  "search_objectives": [
+    {
+      "objective_id": "OBJ-TAIL-SLO-BREACH",
+      "description": "Find workloads that cause p99/p999 scheduling latency to exceed SLO targets.",
+      "target_invariant": "p99_task_latency_us <= 500",
+      "mutation_axes": ["arrival_rate", "burst_size", "cancel_timing"],
+      "severity": "critical"
+    },
+    {
+      "objective_id": "OBJ-FAIRNESS-DRIFT",
+      "description": "Find workloads that cause cancel/ready fairness ratio to drop below threshold.",
+      "target_invariant": "fairness_ratio >= 0.3",
+      "mutation_axes": ["cancel_fraction", "cancel_clustering", "epoch_boundary_timing"],
+      "severity": "critical"
+    },
+    {
+      "objective_id": "OBJ-AUTHORITY-EDGE",
+      "description": "Find workloads that exercise controller promotion/rollback edge cases.",
+      "target_invariant": "no_invalid_state_transitions",
+      "mutation_axes": ["calibration_score_trajectory", "epoch_count", "concurrent_promotions"],
+      "severity": "high"
+    },
+    {
+      "objective_id": "OBJ-RECOVERY-EDGE",
+      "description": "Find workloads that expose recovery path failures or stale fallback states.",
+      "target_invariant": "fallback_clears_after_recovery",
+      "mutation_axes": ["rollback_timing", "concurrent_rollbacks", "hold_release_interleaving"],
+      "severity": "high"
+    },
+    {
+      "objective_id": "OBJ-WAKE-LOSS",
+      "description": "Find workloads that cause lost wakeups under concurrent park/wake races.",
+      "target_invariant": "lost_wakeup_count == 0",
+      "mutation_axes": ["park_wake_interleaving", "bloom_saturation_pressure", "steal_contention"],
+      "severity": "critical"
+    },
+    {
+      "objective_id": "OBJ-OBLIGATION-LEAK",
+      "description": "Find workloads that cause obligation leaks across region boundaries.",
+      "target_invariant": "obligation_leak_count == 0",
+      "mutation_axes": ["region_close_timing", "nested_region_depth", "cancellation_during_settle"],
+      "severity": "critical"
+    },
+    {
+      "objective_id": "OBJ-DIAGNOSABILITY-LOSS",
+      "description": "Find workloads where structured log coverage drops below threshold.",
+      "target_invariant": "log_field_coverage >= 0.9",
+      "mutation_axes": ["log_volume_pressure", "concurrent_log_producers", "field_cardinality"],
+      "severity": "medium"
+    }
+  ],
+  "mutation_model": {
+    "description": "Deterministic, budgeted mutation of replay traces to synthesize challenge workloads.",
+    "mutation_axes": [
+      {
+        "axis_id": "arrival_rate",
+        "description": "Task arrival rate (tasks/sec)",
+        "range": {"min": 1, "max": 100000},
+        "step_modes": ["linear", "geometric", "burst"]
+      },
+      {
+        "axis_id": "cancel_fraction",
+        "description": "Fraction of tasks that are cancelled",
+        "range": {"min": 0.0, "max": 1.0},
+        "step_modes": ["linear"]
+      },
+      {
+        "axis_id": "cancel_timing",
+        "description": "When cancels arrive relative to task start",
+        "range": {"min": 0, "max": 1000},
+        "step_modes": ["uniform", "clustered", "epoch_boundary"]
+      },
+      {
+        "axis_id": "burst_size",
+        "description": "Number of tasks in a single burst",
+        "range": {"min": 1, "max": 10000},
+        "step_modes": ["linear", "exponential"]
+      },
+      {
+        "axis_id": "rollback_timing",
+        "description": "Epoch offset for injected rollback events",
+        "range": {"min": 0, "max": 100},
+        "step_modes": ["uniform", "clustered"]
+      },
+      {
+        "axis_id": "cancel_clustering",
+        "description": "Degree of cancel event temporal clustering",
+        "range": {"min": 0.0, "max": 1.0},
+        "step_modes": ["linear"]
+      },
+      {
+        "axis_id": "epoch_boundary_timing",
+        "description": "Offset of events relative to epoch boundaries",
+        "range": {"min": 0, "max": 100},
+        "step_modes": ["uniform", "boundary_aligned"]
+      },
+      {
+        "axis_id": "calibration_score_trajectory",
+        "description": "Simulated calibration score change over epochs",
+        "range": {"min": 0.0, "max": 1.0},
+        "step_modes": ["linear", "step"]
+      },
+      {
+        "axis_id": "epoch_count",
+        "description": "Number of epochs in the simulation window",
+        "range": {"min": 1, "max": 200},
+        "step_modes": ["linear"]
+      },
+      {
+        "axis_id": "concurrent_promotions",
+        "description": "Number of simultaneous controller promotions",
+        "range": {"min": 1, "max": 10},
+        "step_modes": ["linear"]
+      },
+      {
+        "axis_id": "concurrent_rollbacks",
+        "description": "Number of simultaneous controller rollbacks",
+        "range": {"min": 1, "max": 10},
+        "step_modes": ["linear"]
+      },
+      {
+        "axis_id": "hold_release_interleaving",
+        "description": "Interleaving pattern of hold/release across controllers",
+        "range": {"min": 0, "max": 100},
+        "step_modes": ["uniform", "alternating"]
+      },
+      {
+        "axis_id": "park_wake_interleaving",
+        "description": "Interleaving pattern of worker park/wake events",
+        "range": {"min": 0, "max": 100},
+        "step_modes": ["uniform", "adversarial"]
+      },
+      {
+        "axis_id": "bloom_saturation_pressure",
+        "description": "Pressure on bloom filter capacity to trigger saturation",
+        "range": {"min": 0.0, "max": 1.0},
+        "step_modes": ["linear", "step"]
+      },
+      {
+        "axis_id": "steal_contention",
+        "description": "Number of concurrent stealers targeting the same queue",
+        "range": {"min": 1, "max": 32},
+        "step_modes": ["linear"]
+      },
+      {
+        "axis_id": "region_close_timing",
+        "description": "When region close is triggered relative to obligation settlement",
+        "range": {"min": 0, "max": 1000},
+        "step_modes": ["uniform", "before_settle", "during_settle"]
+      },
+      {
+        "axis_id": "nested_region_depth",
+        "description": "Depth of nested region hierarchy",
+        "range": {"min": 1, "max": 16},
+        "step_modes": ["linear"]
+      },
+      {
+        "axis_id": "cancellation_during_settle",
+        "description": "Inject cancellation during obligation settlement phase",
+        "range": {"min": 0.0, "max": 1.0},
+        "step_modes": ["linear"]
+      },
+      {
+        "axis_id": "log_volume_pressure",
+        "description": "Rate of structured log production relative to capacity",
+        "range": {"min": 0.0, "max": 10.0},
+        "step_modes": ["linear", "burst"]
+      },
+      {
+        "axis_id": "concurrent_log_producers",
+        "description": "Number of concurrent structured log producers",
+        "range": {"min": 1, "max": 64},
+        "step_modes": ["linear"]
+      },
+      {
+        "axis_id": "field_cardinality",
+        "description": "Number of distinct field values in structured logs",
+        "range": {"min": 1, "max": 10000},
+        "step_modes": ["linear", "exponential"]
+      }
+    ],
+    "budget": {
+      "max_mutations_per_run": 1000,
+      "max_wall_clock_seconds": 300,
+      "max_workloads_promoted": 50
+    },
+    "determinism": {
+      "seed_source": "workload_id + objective_id + mutation_round",
+      "reproducibility": "All mutations are deterministic given the seed. No external randomness."
+    }
+  },
+  "challenge_corpus": {
+    "description": "Promoted challenge cases become first-class workload manifests in the corpus.",
+    "promotion_criteria": [
+      "Case is reproducible (deterministic replay succeeds)",
+      "Case is minimized (no redundant mutation axes)",
+      "Case targets a specific objective with measurable violation",
+      "Case has human-readable rationale"
+    ],
+    "manifest_fields": [
+      "challenge_id",
+      "source_workload_id",
+      "objective_id",
+      "mutation_vector",
+      "violation_value",
+      "violation_threshold",
+      "minimization_rounds",

+      "replay_command",
+      "promoted_ts"
+    ],
+    "feedback_targets": [
+      "comparator_tables",
+      "composition_validation",
+      "ci_regression_suite",
+      "closure_artifacts"
+    ]
+  },
+  "structured_log_fields_required": [
+    "search_run_id",
+    "objective_id",
+    "mutation_round",
+    "mutation_vector",
+    "seed",
+    "violation_detected",
+    "violation_value",
+    "violation_threshold",
+    "workload_id",
+    "challenge_id",
+    "promoted",
+    "minimization_rounds",
+    "wall_clock_ms",
+    "budget_remaining"
+  ],
+  "smoke_scenarios": [
+    {
+      "scenario_id": "ARS-SMOKE-OBJECTIVES",
+      "description": "Validate search objective definitions and invariant coverage",
+      "command": "rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa064 cargo test --test adversarial_regime_shift_contract objective -- --nocapture"
+    },
+    {
+      "scenario_id": "ARS-SMOKE-MUTATION",

+    },
+    {
+      "scenario_id": "ARS-SMOKE-CORPUS",
+      "description": "Validate challenge corpus promotion and feedback loop",

@@ -0,0 +1,74 @@
+# Adversarial Regime-Shift Synthesizer Contract
+
+Bead: `asupersync-1508v.6.7`
+
+## Purpose
+
+This contract defines a deterministic, budgeted search that synthesizes worst-case workloads and regime shifts from replay traces, control models, and invariants. Generated challenge cases feed back into the corpus used by the rest of the ascension program, making the runtime adversarially self-hardening.
+
+## Contract Artifacts
+
+1. Canonical artifact: `artifacts/adversarial_regime_shift_v1.json`
+2. Smoke runner: `scripts/run_adversarial_regime_shift_smoke.sh`
+3. Invariant suite: `tests/adversarial_regime_shift_contract.rs`
+
+## Search Objectives
+
+| Objective | Target Invariant | Severity |
+|-----------|-----------------|----------|
+| OBJ-TAIL-SLO-BREACH | p99 latency <= 500us | critical |
+| OBJ-FAIRNESS-DRIFT | fairness_ratio >= 0.3 | critical |
+| OBJ-AUTHORITY-EDGE | no invalid state transitions | high |
+| OBJ-RECOVERY-EDGE | fallback clears after recovery | high |
+| OBJ-WAKE-LOSS | lost_wakeup_count == 0 | critical |
+| OBJ-OBLIGATION-LEAK | obligation_leak_count == 0 | critical |
+| OBJ-DIAGNOSABILITY-LOSS | log_field_coverage >= 0.9 | medium |
+
+Each objective specifies mutation axes (what to vary), a target invariant (what to violate), and severity (how urgently a violation must be addressed).
+
+## Mutation Model
+
+Mutations are deterministic: given a seed derived from `workload_id + objective_id + mutation_round`, all mutations are reproducible. The search operates under explicit budgets:
+
+- Max mutations per run: 1000
+- Max wall-clock time: 300 seconds
+- Max workloads promoted: 50
+
+Mutation axes include arrival rate, cancel fraction/timing, burst size, rollback timing, park/wake interleaving, bloom saturation pressure, region close timing, and others.
+
+## Challenge Corpus Feedback
+
+Promoted challenge cases must satisfy:
+
+1. Reproducible (deterministic replay succeeds)
+2. Minimized (no redundant mutation axes)
+3. Targeted (specific objective with measurable violation)
+4. Documented (human-readable rationale)
+
+Promoted cases feed into: comparator tables, composition validation, CI regression suite, and closure artifacts.
+
+## Structured Logging Contract
+
+Synthesis logs MUST include: `search_run_id`, `objective_id`, `mutation_round`, `mutation_vector`, `seed`, `violation_detected`, `violation_value`, `violation_threshold`, `workload_id`, `challenge_id`, `promoted`, `minimization_rounds`, `wall_clock_ms`, `budget_remaining`.
+
+## Comparator-Smoke Runner
+
+```bash
+bash ./scripts/run_adversarial_regime_shift_smoke.sh --list
+bash ./scripts/run_adversarial_regime_shift_smoke.sh --execute
+```
+
+## Validation
+
+```bash
+rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa064 cargo test --test adversarial_regime_shift_contract -- --nocapture
+```
+
+## Cross-References
+
+- `artifacts/adversarial_regime_shift_v1.json`
+- `artifacts/runtime_workload_corpus_v1.json` -- Source replay corpus
+- `artifacts/digital_twin_v1.json` -- Twin stages for simulation
+- `artifacts/runtime_control_seam_inventory_v1.json` -- Seam IDs
+- `artifacts/bounded_controller_synthesis_v1.json` -- Controller domains
+- `artifacts/replay_minimization_validation_contract_v1.json` -- Minimization

@@ -0,0 +1,136 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+# Schema anchors for contract invariants:
+# - adversarial-regime-shift-smoke-bundle-v1
+# - adversarial-regime-shift-smoke-run-report-v1
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
+CONTRACT_ARTIFACT="${PROJECT_ROOT}/artifacts/adversarial_regime_shift_v1.json"
+OUTPUT_ROOT="${ARS_SMOKE_OUTPUT_DIR:-${PROJECT_ROOT}/target/adversarial-regime-shift-smoke}"
+TIMESTAMP="$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"
+RUN_DIR="${OUTPUT_ROOT}/run_${TIMESTAMP}"
+LIST_ONLY=0
+DRY_RUN=1
+
+declare -a SELECTED_SCENARIOS=()
+
+usage() {
+    cat <<'USAGE'
+Usage: ./scripts/run_adversarial_regime_shift_smoke.sh [options]
+
+Options:
+  --list                    List scenario IDs and exit
+  --scenario <id>           Run one scenario (repeatable)
+  --output-root <dir>       Override output root
+  --dry-run                 Emit manifests without executing (default)
+  --execute                 Execute cargo test scenarios
+  -h, --help                Show help
+USAGE
+}
+
+require_tools() {
+    if ! command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        echo "FATAL: jq is required" >&2; exit 1
+    fi
+    if [ ! -f "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT" ]; then
+        echo "FATAL: contract artifact missing at ${CONTRACT_ARTIFACT}" >&2; exit 1
+    fi
+}
+
+json_escape() { printf '%s' "$1" | sed 's/\\/\\\\/g; s/"/\\"/g'; }
+contract_version() { jq -r '.contract_version' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"; }
+bundle_schema_version() { jq -r '.runner_bundle_schema_version' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"; }
+report_schema_version() { jq -r '.runner_report_schema_version' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"; }
+
+list_scenarios() {
+    jq -r '.smoke_scenarios[] | [.scenario_id, .description] | @tsv' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT" \
+        | while IFS=$'\t' read -r sid desc; do printf '%-38s %s\n' "$sid" "$desc"; done
+}
+
+load_scenario_json() {
+    jq -c --arg sid "$1" '.smoke_scenarios[] | select(.scenario_id == $sid)' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"
+}
+
+RESULTS_JSON=""
+append_result() {
+    if [[ -z "$RESULTS_JSON" ]]; then RESULTS_JSON="$1"; else RESULTS_JSON="${RESULTS_JSON},$1"; fi
+}
+
+run_scenario() {
+    local sid="$1" scenario_json description command scenario_dir log_file summary_file started_ts ended_ts status rc
+    scenario_json="$(load_scenario_json "$sid")"
+    [[ -z "$scenario_json" ]] && { echo "FATAL: unknown scenario: $sid" >&2; return 1; }
+    description="$(jq -r '.description' <<<"$scenario_json")"
+    command="$(jq -r '.command' <<<"$scenario_json")"
+    scenario_dir="${RUN_DIR}/${sid}"; log_file="${scenario_dir}/run.log"; summary_file="${scenario_dir}/bundle_manifest.json"
+    mkdir -p "$scenario_dir"
+    started_ts="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
+    echo ">>> Running scenario ${sid}"
+    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ]]; then
+        printf 'DRY_RUN scenario=%s\n' "$sid" >"$log_file"; rc=0; status="dry_run"
+    else
+        rc=0; eval "$command" >"$log_file" 2>&1 || rc=$?
+        status="$( [[ "$rc" -eq 0 ]] && printf "passed" || printf "failed" )"
+    fi
+    ended_ts="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
+    cat >"$summary_file" <<JSON
+{
+  "schema_version": "$(json_escape "$(bundle_schema_version)")",
+  "contract_version": "$(json_escape "$(contract_version)")",
+  "scenario_id": "$(json_escape "$sid")",
+  "description": "$(json_escape "$description")",
+  "status": "$(json_escape "$status")",
+  "exit_code": ${rc},
+  "started_ts": "$(json_escape "$started_ts")",
+  "ended_ts": "$(json_escape "$ended_ts")"
+}
+JSON
+    append_result "$(jq -c '.' "$summary_file")"
+    [[ "$rc" -eq 0 ]]
+}
+
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+    case "$1" in
+        --list) LIST_ONLY=1; shift ;;
+        --scenario) SELECTED_SCENARIOS+=("${2:-}"); shift 2 ;;
+        --output-root) OUTPUT_ROOT="${2:-}"; RUN_DIR="${OUTPUT_ROOT}/run_${TIMESTAMP}"; shift 2 ;;
+        --dry-run) DRY_RUN=1; shift ;;
+        --execute) DRY_RUN=0; shift ;;
+        -h|--help) usage; exit 0 ;;
+        *) echo "Unknown: $1" >&2; usage >&2; exit 1 ;;
+    esac
+done
+
+require_tools
+if [[ "$LIST_ONLY" -eq 1 ]]; then list_scenarios; exit 0; fi
+if [[ "${#SELECTED_SCENARIOS[@]}" -eq 0 ]]; then
+    mapfile -t SELECTED_SCENARIOS < <(jq -r '.smoke_scenarios[].scenario_id' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT")
+fi
+
+mkdir -p "$RUN_DIR"; OVERALL_RC=0
+for sid in "${SELECTED_SCENARIOS[@]}"; do run_scenario "$sid" || OVERALL_RC=1; done
+
+RUN_REPORT="${RUN_DIR}/run_report.json"
+cat >"$RUN_REPORT" <<JSON
+{
+  "schema_version": "$(json_escape "$(report_schema_version)")",
+  "contract_version": "$(json_escape "$(contract_version)")",
+  "run_dir": "$(json_escape "$RUN_DIR")",
+  "dry_run": $( [[ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ]] && printf 'true' || printf 'false' ),
+  "results": [${RESULTS_JSON}],
+  "status": "$([ "$OVERALL_RC" -eq 0 ] && printf "passed" || printf "failed")"
+}
+JSON
+
+echo ""
+echo "==================================================================="
+echo "     ADVERSARIAL REGIME-SHIFT SMOKE SUMMARY                        "
+echo "==================================================================="
+echo "  Run dir:   ${RUN_DIR}"
+echo "  Mode:      $([ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ] && printf "DRY-RUN" || printf "EXECUTE")"
+echo "  Status:    $([ "$OVERALL_RC" -eq 0 ] && printf "PASSED" || printf "FAILED")"
+echo "==================================================================="
+
+exit "$OVERALL_RC"
